d: To register your arrival at an airport, hotel, or event
e: "You need to check in two hours before your flight."

d: Bags small enough to bring onto the plane cabin
e: "I only have carry-on luggage — no checked bags."

d: The document that lets you board your flight
e: "Please have your boarding pass ready."

d: The specific door at the airport where you board your plane
e: "Your departure gate is B14."

d: A stop between flights, usually a few hours
e: "We have a three-hour layover in Tokyo."

d: The government authority that checks what you bring into a country
e: "You must declare all goods at customs."

d: The airport screening process for banned items
e: "Going through security took 40 minutes."

d: To reserve something ahead of time
e: "Book your hotel in advance during peak season."

d: A ticket for travel to a destination and back
e: "I need a round trip to Paris, please."

d: A ticket for travel to a destination only, no return
e: "Just a one-way ticket — I'm moving there."

d: How much one currency is worth in another
e: "Check the exchange rate before converting money."

d: The money used in the country you're visiting
e: "It's better to pay in local currency."

d: To add money to a card or phone plan
e: "I need to top up my transit card."

d: Visiting famous or interesting places as a tourist
e: "We spent the whole day sightseeing."

d: A place that is not famous or crowded; hidden gem
e: "That restaurant is totally off the beaten path — locals only."

d: Tiredness and disorientation after a long flight across time zones
e: "I'm still recovering from jet lag."

d: Free of charge, provided as a courtesy
e: "Breakfast is complimentary for all guests."

d: An available room in a hotel
e: "Do you have any vacancy for this weekend?"

d: Leaving a hotel room later than the standard checkout time
e: "Can I request a late checkout? My flight is at 6 PM."

d: To travel to different places within an area
e: "The best way to get around the city is by metro."

d: Describing a place that is interesting enough to go to
e: "The old town is definitely worth a visit."

d: Insurance that covers medical and trip-related emergencies abroad
e: "Always buy travel insurance for long trips."

d: A second flight you take to reach your final destination
e: "I missed my connecting flight in Chicago."

d: To book a table at a restaurant or room at a hotel
e: "I'd like to make a reservation for two at 7pm."

d: A period when a place has fewer tourists and lower prices
e: "Travel off season to save money."

d: A seat next to the walkway on a plane or train
e: "I prefer an aisle seat so I can stretch."

d: A seat next to the window
e: "She booked a window seat for the views."

d: A small bag you take into the cabin
e: "Your carry-on must fit in the overhead bin."

d: Luggage stored in the hold, not the cabin
e: "Checked baggage costs extra on this airline."

d: Border control where your passport and visa are checked
e: "The immigration line was really long."

d: A ticket that includes the return journey
e: "A round-trip ticket is cheaper than two one-ways."

d: A ticket for travel in one direction only
e: "I only need a one-way to London."

d: A planned schedule of a trip
e: "Send me the itinerary so I know the plan."

d: A place or service to swap one currency for another
e: "I changed money at the currency exchange."

d: A place that overcharges tourists
e: "That restaurant is a total tourist trap."

d: A tour led by a knowledgeable guide
e: "The guided tour explained the history well."

d: Cheap, shared budget accommodation
e: "We stayed in a hostel to save money."

d: To leave and pay at a hotel
e: "We need to check out before 11 a.m."

d: Food delivered to your hotel room
e: "We ordered room service after the long flight."

d: A phone call to wake you at a set time
e: "Can I get a wake-up call at six?"

d: Having no rooms or seats left
e: "The hotel was fully booked for the festival."

d: An item bought to remember a trip
e: "I brought back a small souvenir for you."

d: A tip for good service
e: "A gratuity is often added for large groups."

d: The traditional food of a place
e: "Try the local cuisine while you are here."

d: Food sold by vendors on the street
e: "The street food here is amazing and cheap."

d: A famous, easily recognized place
e: "The tower is the city's best-known landmark."

d: An official document for international travel
e: "Don't forget your passport!"

d: Official permission to enter a country
e: "You need a visa to visit."

d: A building where flights depart and arrive
e: "Our flight leaves from Terminal 2."

d: Where you collect checked luggage
e: "Meet me at baggage claim."

d: An overnight flight
e: "We took the red-eye to save a day."

d: With meals and drinks included
e: "We booked an all-inclusive resort."

d: The busiest, most expensive travel time
e: "Flights cost more in peak season."
